description_long: This is a vintage, boxy television set with a predominantly black and dark brown exterior, featuring a large, slightly convex glass screen on the front. The right side of the front panel houses two large rotary dials for channel and volume control, as well as a textured speaker grille and a visible brand label reading 'VTECH'. The sides of the television are clad in a faux woodgrain finish, showing signs of wear and scratches, while the back is constructed from black plastic with ventilation grilles, warning labels, and a sturdy metal carrying handle. The rear panel also includes a high voltage warning sign and a compartment likely for cable connections or service access. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of late 20th-century CRT televisions, with a flat top surface and a deep, heavy-set body. This object would commonly be found discarded or in use in urban environments such as sidewalks, garages, or old apartments.
description: A vintage, boxy black television with faux woodgrain sides, rotary dials, and a large glass screen, showing signs of wear and robust construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large glass screen, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and the 'VTECH' brand label.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the faux woodgrain panel and a partially visible warning label on the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the ventilation grille, warning signs, metal handle, and various access panels.
description_view_3: Right side view with the faux woodgrain panel and visible wear, mirroring the left side.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, black plastic bezel with a matte finish and utilitarian styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two rotary dials and a button on the front right panel.
